name: Marketing Podcast Episode
description: Turn a week's topics into a Made in mChatAI podcast episode: show notes (markdown) + spoken script + TTS audio file. Ready to upload to GCS and add to mchataiweb/public/podcast-content/_index.json.

config.userPrompt:
You are writing show notes for the Made in mChatAI podcast (weekly, 15-25 min, hosted by Lawrence Norman). Audience: indie creators and prosumer Mac users who build games, tools, podcasts, and automations using AI. Voice: warm, direct, helpful. ASCII only.

Given the topic or week's notes below, produce structured show notes in markdown.

Required sections (use these exact H2 headings):

## Cold open
2-3 sentences. The hook that earns the first 30 seconds.

## This week's build
5-7 minutes worth. What got made, the prompt used, the surprising moment, the link to play it.

## Recipe of the week
5-7 minutes. One agentic recipe explained: setup, what it does, what it unlocks. Name the integrations honestly (no marketing names invented).

## Community spotlight
2-3 minutes. One creator's published mini-app from the Hub, plus their prompt and what's clever about it.

## What's shipping next
2-3 minutes. Concrete: name the feature, give an honest ETA range, or say 'no ETA yet'. No promises.

## Links mentioned
Bullet list of every URL referenced.

Week's topic / raw notes:
{{INPUT}}

ASCII only  -  no smart quotes, no em-dashes, no curly apostrophes. Output the show notes only, no preamble.

Convert these show notes into a natural spoken script for a single-host podcast. ASCII only.

Rules:
- This will be read by text-to-speech, so write only what should be spoken.
- No markdown, no headings, no bullet points, no bracketed stage directions.
- Use natural transitions between segments ('Up next', 'A quick note before we move on', 'And one more thing this week').
- Spell out numbers under ten. Spell out abbreviations on first mention.
- For URLs, say 'visit m-chat-a-i dot com slash recipes', not the raw URL.
- Use contractions naturally (it's, you're, that's).
- Open with: 'Welcome back to Made in mChatAI. I'm Lawrence Norman.'
- Close with: 'Thanks for listening. Until next week.'
- Length target: 2000-3000 spoken words (12-18 minutes at normal pace).
- Keep the energy steady; this is a conversational tech show, not a hype reel.

SHOW NOTES:
{{INPUT}}
